title: Sr. Staff Systems Engineer
description_text: At Wisk, we're transforming the future of urban mobility through safe, all-electric, autonomous flight. We are a passionate team working together toward a sustainable future, solving high-impact problems that have never been solved before. By delivering everyday flight for everyone, we're making it possible to spend less time getting there and more time being there. If you want to be part of shaping the future of mobility, then read on! We are looking for an Sr. Staff Aircraft Systems Engineer to join our team. The goal of a Sr. Staff Aircraft Systems Engineer at Wisk is to ensure that the aircraft is designed, built and verified for intended functionality. You will be part of the Aircraft Systems Engineering, Integration and test team that drive Aircraft Integration Activities. This is a hybrid work opportunity. At Wisk, this means working onsite at our Mountain View office 3 days per week with flexibility for the remainder of the days. Responsibilities As a senior member of the team, you will support and drive definition and development activities supporting artifacts required for ARP4754 and ARP 4761 compliance. You will review safety critical system integration activities based on today's best system engineering practices, lessons learned and guidelines. You will support and drive the multiple trade studies needed to achieve the most efficient and optimized safety critical systems architectural solutions that meet performance, size, weight, power and safety requirements as applicable from the FAA/EASA regulations. As an integrator, you will own and drive the schedule and end-to-end integration activities that range across mass, power, regulatory compliance, PRAs, top technical issues, to name a few. As a member in the SEIT organization, in collaboration with other leaders, you will contribute towards development of efficient and optimized strategies for execution, to be implemented across multiple IPTs. You will support the system development and validation efforts that support the development, integration, testing of the integrated aircraft system in support of flight test and certification. You will be detailed and technically oriented and have the ability to work proactively and effectively with minimal supervision, with the ability to prioritize and multi-task in a fast paced and challenging environment. As a member in the SEIT organization, you will exemplify the culture, mission, values, vision and quality policy of the organization Requirements B.S. in engineering and 12 years experience or M.S in engineering with 8 years experience in Aircraft Integration, Systems Engineering or related field Hands on experience in development and decomposition of requirements, developing and allocating functions, and interfacing with certification and systems safety teams to deliver certification deliverables. Expertise with ARP4754A, DO-160, ARP4761, Part 23/25, regulations, AC XX.1309, or similar standards Familiarity with DO-178C, DO-254. Experience in requirements management tools such as Polarion, Doors, Teamcenter Requirements management, Requiem etc. Excellent communication and collaboration skills Previous system development, design, manufacturing, or flight test experience Experience in development of one or more of the following: Complex, highly coupled aircraft systems, Avionics systems, Flight Controls, Propulsion Systems Job Type: Full-time Pay range the company expects to pay: $193,000 - $241,000 per year - The starting base salary within this range for this role varies based on factors such as your geographical location, and your relevant job-related experience that is consistent with a business necessity. Wisk HQ is located in Mountain View just a couple of hundred feet from Shoreline Lake and its surrounding walking/jogging trails. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, and life insurance, 9 off Fridays for full-time employees throughout the year, flexible vacation time off, 11 company holidays, a 401(k) plan with 50% company matching vested immediately, commuter assistance, and education assistance. And on top of all that, we also offer a variety of perks such as free catered lunches, flight lessons, wellness allowances, snacks, drinks, employee referral bonus, charitable giving match, patent bonus program, and more. title: Software Engineer, Autonomy Integration
description_text: At Wisk, we're transforming the future of urban mobility through safe, all-electric, autonomous flight. We are a passionate team working together toward a sustainable future, solving high-impact problems that have never been solved before. By delivering everyday flight for everyone, we're making it possible to spend less time getting there and more time being there. If you want to be part of shaping the future of mobility, then read on! We are looking for a Software Engineer, Autonomy Integration to join our team. The goal of a Software Engineer , Autonomy Integration at Wisk is to ensure that autonomy modules and simulation environments are seamlessly deployed, integrated, and scaled to meet rigorous engineering milestones. You will be part of the Autonomy Simulation team that delivers the core software frameworks and testing infrastructures required to validate our autonomous aircraft. This position is Remote in the US and will require travel as needed. Responsibilities As part of the Simulation organization, you will act as a Software Engineer, Simulation Integration for a novel type of aircraft development targeting the autonomous UAM market. You will take part in autonomy module development, SimFra environment deployment, and use-case-related integration of modules. You will support engineering code owners and stakeholders by facilitating direct code integration, troubleshooting environment issues, and streamlining deployments. In some areas, you will be working closely with cross-functional autonomy and validation teams to ensure complex environments and builds are configured correctly for testing milestones. As a Software Engineer , Simulation Integration, you will help define and develop best practices for simulation integration, configuration management, and build processes. Requirements B.S. in Computer Science, Aerospace Engineering, or a relevant engineering discipline with at least 2–5 years of experience in software development, simulation modeling, or systems integration You have worked in aerospace, robotics, autonomous vehicles, or a highly regulated tech industry. You have hands-on experience developing software models, managing environmental deployments, and integrating complex modules using Python, C++, and Go. You have a deep and applied knowledge of software integration workflows, version control, and deploying setups within framework-driven simulation architectures (such as SimFra). You are familiar with JS/TS, Matlab, Simulink, and building automated pipelines to support robust software deployment. This role will require less than 10% of travel. Job Type: Full-time Pay range the company expects to pay: $115,000 - $144,000 per year - The starting base salary within this range for this role varies based on factors such as your geographical location, and your relevant job-related experience that is consistent with a business necessity. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, and life insurance, 9 off Fridays for full-time employees throughout the year, flexible vacation time off, 11 company holidays, a 401(k) plan with 50% company matching vested immediately, commuter assistance, and education assistance. And on top of all that, we also offer a variety of perks such as free catered lunches, flight lessons, wellness allowances, snacks, drinks, employee referral bonus, charitable giving match, patent bonus program, and more. title: Professionnel Senior - Intégration de la simulation / Senior Professional - Simulation Integration
description_text: Chez Wisk, nous transformons l'avenir de la mobilité urbaine en proposant des vols sécuritaires, entièrement électriques et autonomes. Nous sommes une équipe passionnée qui travaille ensemble pour un avenir durable, en résolvant des problèmes à fort impact qui n'ont jamais été résolus auparavant. En proposant des vols quotidiens à portée de tous, nous faisons en sorte qu'il soit possible de passer moins de temps dans les transports et plus de temps à profiter. Si vous voulez faire partie du futur de la mobilité, poursuivez la lecture! Nous recherchons un Professionnel Senior en intégration de simulation pour garantir que les systèmes de simulation soient correctement intégrés et déployés sur divers actifs de développement et de test situés à Laval, Mountain View et Fremont. Ces simulations incluent, sans s'y limiter : Simulations en temps réel, de type « Hardware-In-the-Loop » (HIL) et au niveau système Modèles logiciels, réhébergement (Rehost), re-ciblage (Retarget), LSAP et logiciels embarqués Systèmes aéronautiques Systèmes d'autonomie En raison de la nature du travail requis pour ce poste, vous devrez travailler en mode hybride depuis nos bureaux de Laval. Chez Wisk, le mode hybride signifie travailler au bureau 3 jours par semaine, avec une flexibilité pour les jours restants. Responsabilités Maintenir, intégrer, déployer et valider des simulations avancées pour un ensemble diversifié de simulateurs. Soutenir toutes les activités d'intégration matériel-logiciel et de mise en service des actifs de simulation et de test. Enquêter sur les solutions de simulation déployées et en assurer le dépannage, en particulier sur les actifs de simulation et de test intégrés. Veiller à ce que les rapports d'anomalies (SNAGs) signalés sur les actifs de simulation et de test intégrés soient correctement documentés et traités. Garantir que la configuration des actifs de simulation et de test intégrés soit contrôlée et précise en permanence. Développer et déployer des correctifs logiciels pour les simulations (selon la pertinence et la faisabilité). Contribuer au développement de l'expertise du département en intégration de simulations. Collaborer avec les clients internes et externes pour définir les exigences relatives aux systèmes de simulation et aux actifs de test, planifier les jalons et fournir des solutions de simulation critiques pour soutenir le développement des produits. Travailler avec des équipes agiles pour garantir que les besoins des parties prenantes internes et externes soient satisfaits. Veuillez noter que des déplacements sur site seront requis pour soutenir la mise en service et le maintien en condition opérationnelle des actifs de simulation et de test intégrés. Exigences Baccalauréat dans un domaine de l'ingénierie, avec 5 ans d'expérience pertinente. Minimum de 3 ans d'expérience combinée en intégration matériel-logiciel et/ou expérience concrète dans le développement de simulations sur des dispositifs HIL. Maîtrise du développement logiciel, de préférence avec C/C++, Python et Matlab/Simulink, et expérience des protocoles de communication. Solides compétences en dépannage et en intégration matériel-logiciel. Approche créative de la résolution de problèmes. Capacité à travailler efficacement dans un environnement dynamique, avec des besoins et exigences en constante évolution. Excellentes compétences en organisation et en gestion du temps. Excellentes compétences en communication, tant à l'écrit qu'à l'oral. Ce poste peut nécessiter jusqu'à 20 % de déplacements durant certaines phases de projet (2 semaines sur 2 mois) Un atout si vous possédez : Une familiarité avec l'avionique, les systèmes, la dynamique de vol ou les opérations aéronautiques. Une familiarité avec les protocoles de communication/avionique aéronautiques courants, tels qu'ARINC 429 et ARINC 664. Une expérience préalable dans la conception, le développement, les essais et la certification de dispositifs d'entraînement à la simulation de vol (FSTD) ou d'actifs intégrés de simulation et d'essais (ex. : station d'intégration et d'essais systèmes, « Iron Bird », etc.). Une expérience préalable dans la modélisation, la stimulation et/ou l'intégration d'au moins l'un des aspects suivants de la simulation aérospatiale : Avionique (FMS, EGPWS, WXR, TCAS, etc.) Systèmes avion (pneumatique, électrique, hydraulique, etc.) Dynamique de vol (aérodynamique, stabilité, commandes de vol, etc.) Liaisons de commandement et de contrôle (C2) Systèmes de contrôle du trafic aérien Systèmes de communication (ACARS, CPDLC, etc.) Systèmes GPS/de navigation Une familiarité avec les normes ARINC 708, ARINC 615, ARINC 610, MIL-STD-1553, MIL-STD-1760, FCB, le bus CAN ou RS485. Une familiarité avec les technologies cloud (GCP, AWS, Azure) ainsi qu'avec les services et applications web. Nous offrons un ensemble complet d'avantages incluant une assurance médicale, dentaire, visuelle et une assurance-vie (prise en charge à 100 % de la prime pour les employés par l’entreprise), 9 vendredis libres pour les employés à temps plein tout au long de l'année, 20 jours de congés payés, 10 jours fériés, un plan d’épargne-retraite avec une contribution de l’entreprise à 100 % jusqu'à 8 % de vos revenus admissibles et une aide à la formation. En plus, nous proposons divers avantages, tels que des leçons de pilotage, des allocations bien-être, des collations, des boissons, un bonus pour recommandation d’employés, une contribution aux dons de charité, un programme de bonus pour les brevets, et bien plus encore. La maîtrise de l'anglais est requise pour ce poste afin de développer notre produit et de servir nos clients, dont la majorité ou la totalité se trouvent à l'extérieur du Québec, ainsi que pour communiquer avec des partenaires et des collègues situés aux États-Unis, en Pologne, en Australie, en Nouvelle-Zélande et au Canada. Les informations techniques provenant de ces pays sont disponibles en anglais seulement. Une connaissance fonctionnelle du français est requise pour les postes situés en permanence au Québec afin que le titulaire puisse communiquer avec ses collègues et partenaires au Québec au besoin. Une formation en français est offerte à tout titulaire d'un poste permanent au Québec qui ne possède pas une bonne connaissance du français. À toutes les agences de recrutement : Wisk Aero n’accepte pas les curriculum vitae des agences. Veuillez ne pas envoyer de curriculum vitae à notre alias d’emploi, aux employés de Wisk Aero ou à tout autre emplacement de l’organisation. Wisk Aero n’est pas responsable des frais liés aux CV non sollicités. At Wisk, we're transforming the future of urban mobility through safe, all-electric, autonomous flight. We are a passionate team working together toward a sustainable future, solving high-impact problems that have never been solved before. By delivering everyday flight for everyone, we're making it possible to spend less time getting there and more time being there. If you want to be part of shaping the future of mobility, then read on! We are looking for a Simulation Integration Specialist to ensure that simulation systems are correctly integrated and deployed on various development and test assets located in Laval, Mountain View and Fremont. These simulations include, but are not limited to: Real-time, Hardware-In-the-Loop (HIL), and System-Level Software model, Rehost, Retarget, LSAP and Embedded Software Aircraft Systems Autonomy Systems Due to the nature of the work required for this position, you will be required to work in hybrid mode from our Laval office. At Wisk, hybrid means working in the office 3 days per week with flexibility for the remainder of the days. Responsibilities Carry out the maintenance, integration, deployment and validation of advanced simulations for a diverse set of simulators. Support all hardware-software integration and commissioning activities for simulation and test assets. Investigate and troubleshoot deployed simulation solutions; particularly on integrated simulation and test assets. Ensure that discrepancy reports (SNAGs) raised against the integrated simulation and test assets are appropriately documented and dispositioned. Ensure that the configuration of the integrated simulation and test assets are controlled and accurate at all times. Develop and deploy simulation software fixes (as appropriate and feasible). Help to cultivate and develop departmental expertise with simulation integration. Collaborate with internal and external customers to identify simulation system/test asset requirements, plan milestones and deliver critical simulation solutions to support product development. Work with agile teams to ensure that the needs of internal and external stakeholders are met. Note that travel to onsite location to support the commissioning and sustainment of the integrated simulation and test assets will be required. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in an engineering discipline with 5 years of relevant experience. Minimum 3 years of combined experience in hardware/software integration and/or of hands-on experience developing simulations on HIL Devices. Proficiency with software development, preferably using C/C++, Python, Matlab/Simulink. Experience with communication protocols Strong hardware/software integration troubleshooting skills Creative approach to problem solving Ability to work effectively in a dynamic environment with changing needs and requirements Excellent organizational and time management skills Excellent written and verbal communication skills This role might require up to 20% travel in certain project phases (2w/2m) Great if you have: Familiarity with aircraft avionics, aircraft systems, aircraft flight dynamics, or aircraft operations. Familiarity with common aircraft communication/avionics protocols such as ARINC 429 and ARINC 664. Prior experience with the design, development, test and certification of Flight Simulation Training Devices (FSTDs) or of integrated simulation and test assets (i.e. System Integration Test Station, Iron Bird, etc.). Prior experience with the modeling, stimulation and/or integration of at least one of the following aspects of aerospace related simulation: Aircraft Avionics (FMS, EGPWS, WXR, TCAS etc) Aircraft Systems (pneumatics, electrics, hydraulics, etc) Aircraft Flight Dynamics (aerodynamics, stability, flight controls, etc) Command and Control (C2) links Air Traffic Control Systems Communication Systems (ACARS, CPDLC, etc) GPS/Navigation Systems Familiarity with ARINC 708, ARINC 615, ARINC 610, MIL-STD-1553, MIL-STD-1760, FCB, CAN bus or RS485. Familiarity with cloud technologies (GCP, AWS, Azure) and web services/applications. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, and life insurance (company pays 100% of employee premium), 9 off Fridays for full time employees throughout the year, 20 days of PTO, 10 company holidays, a retirement savings plan with 100% company matching up to 8% of your eligible earnings and education assistance. And on top of all that, we also offer a variety of perks such as flight lessons, wellness allowances, snacks, drinks, employee referral bonus, charitable giving match, patent bonus program, and more. English is required for this position in order to develop our product and eventually serve our customers, most or all of whom are located outside Quebec, as well as to communicate with partners and colleagues located in the United States, Poland, Australia, New Zealand, and Canada. The technical information from these countries is available in English only. Functional knowledge of French is required for positions permanently located in Quebec so incumbents can communicate with their colleagues and partners in Quebec as necessary. French-language training is offered to all incumbents in permanent positions in Quebec who do not have a good knowledge of French. To all recruitment agencies: Wisk Aero does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Wisk Aero employees, or any other organization location. Wisk Aero is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

title: Quality Engineer
description_text: At Wisk, we're transforming the future of urban mobility through safe, all-electric, autonomous flight. We are a passionate team working together toward a sustainable future, solving high-impact problems that have never been solved before. By delivering everyday flight for everyone, we're making it possible to spend less time getting there and more time being there. If you want to be part of shaping the future of mobility, then read on! We are seeking a Quality Engineer to join our Enterprise Quality & Safety team at our manufacturing site in Mountain View, CA. In this role, you will serve as the primary representative for our final integration team, ensuring that quality is integrated into the design and production of our autonomous, all-electric aircraft. You will be responsible for defining and maintaining the quality standards necessary for flight-critical hardware, moving beyond passive support to drive systemic improvements in our manufacturing and inspection processes. Due to the nature of the work required for this position, this is an onsite opportunity. You will be required to work from our Mountain View office. You will collaborate across Integrated Product Teams (IPTs), including engineering and supply chain, to lead QMS implementation, conduct FMEAs, and ensure strict FAA compliance throughout the lifecycle of the eVTOL program. By leveraging Google Workspace, Power BI, and the Atlassian suite, you will manage data-driven quality systems and lead complex problem-solving through RCCA and 5-Why methodologies. You will utilize your experience with powertrain systems and metrology tools, such as CMMs and laser trackers, to validate hardware integrity and ensure all manufacturing outputs meet engineering intent. . Responsibilities As part of the Enterprise Safety & Quality organization, you will serve as the primary interface for our propulsion and machine shops, ensuring all activities align with the Quality Management System (QMS). You will partner with Integrated Product Teams (IPTs) to establish, implement, and enforce the quality requirements and tools required throughout the lifecycle of the eVTOL program. You will lead complex problem-solving activities through RCCA, applying methodologies such as 5 Whys and Ishikawa diagrams to optimize inspection techniques and streamline production. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to drive QMS implementation, lead Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA), and manage other critical quality processes. You will ensure all manufacturing and engineering activities maintain compliance with FAA regulatory requirements and certification standards. Requirements Bachelor of Science in Engineering (Aeronautical, Mechanical, or related discipline). 2+ years of Quality or Manufacturing Engineering experience within the aerospace, automotive or safety-critical sectors. Experience with metallic and composite components and validating flight hardware inspectability using metrology tools such as CMM machines and laser trackers. Proven ability to interpret and critique design drawings, process specifications, and autoclave reports to ensure adherence to safety standards. Basic knowledge with Geometric Dimensioning and Tolerancing (GD&T) practices. A strong and applied knowledge of manufacturing practices, product development and design, quality systems and tools. Participated and Led Root Cause Corrective Action (RCCA) processes using industry-standard methodologies (e.g., 8D, Fishbone, 5 Whys) to eliminate recurring non-conformances. Desired: 5 or more years of quality engineering experience, ideally in the aviation domain. 1-2 year experience with sub-assemblies and AS9100 manufacturing/quality standards. ASQ Certified Quality Engineer (CQE), Six Sigma Green belt or equivalent. Experience using the Atlassian suite, specifically Jira for issue tracking and Confluence for documentation management. title: Senior Systems Engineer
description_text: At Wisk, we're transforming the future of urban mobility through safe, all-electric, autonomous flight. We are a passionate team working together toward a sustainable future, solving high-impact problems that have never been solved before. By delivering everyday flight for everyone, we're making it possible to spend less time getting there and more time being there. If you want to be part of shaping the future of mobility, then read on! We are looking for a Senior Systems Engineer to join our team. The goal of a Systems Engineer focused on our Ground Control Station at Wisk is to ensure all aspects of the Flight Test Ground Control Station and Airborne Comms hardware and software are developed and maintained in support of the Wisk Gen 6 flight test campaign. You will be part of the Avionics team that is responsible for the overall development and integration of the avionics on the Gen 6 aircraft. In this role you will have the opportunity to leave a lasting impact on aviation by designing and developing systems for an autonomous passenger carrying eVTOL service by defining systems required to communicate with our aircraft, deliver C2-link airborne avionics software and ground infrastructure and be responsible for the collection and distribution of data between our aircraft and ground systems. You will also define the systems which support integration and test of the Flight Test Ground Control software required for the flight test campaign. This is a hybrid work opportunity. At Wisk, this means working onsite at our Mountain View office 3 days per week with flexibility for the remainder of the days. Responsibilities Responsible for delivering high quality Systems Engineering artifacts for a novel type of aircraft development targeting the autonomous UAM market. You will participate in design and implementation of safety-critical and non-safety-critical systems related to the Ground Control Station and Airborne Communications Systems Support the system requirements development,validation, and testing/verification efforts for all GCS Products You define and document the technical solution and integration efforts with other projects and external partners including the flight test team and aircraft software development team Support and contribute to a high performing Systems Engineering team to develop functions, architecture, requirements, integrate between system interfaces and develop process strategy. Detailed and technically oriented and have the ability to work proactively and effectively with minimal supervision, with the ability to prioritize and multitask in a fast paced and challenging environment. Review safety critical system integration activities based on today's best system engineering practices, lessons learned and guidelines. Support a team that will support the multiple trade studies needed to achieve the most efficient and optimized safety critical systems architectural solutions that meet performance, size, weight, power and safety requirements as applicable from the FAA/EASA regulations. Your goal is to make sure that designs are manufacturable and build the aircraft of the future. The successful candidate is knowledgeable and experienced with advanced industrial manufacturing and has direct hands-on experience developing and building type certified aircraft or working in regulated environments. In this role, the candidate must be proactive, forward thinking, and self-motivated with the ability to effectively communicate information and ideas with a cross functional and diverse team. title: Principal Software Development Engineer
description_text: At Wisk, we're transforming the future of urban mobility through safe, all-electric, autonomous flight. We are a passionate team working together toward a sustainable future, solving high-impact problems that have never been solved before. By delivering everyday flight for everyone, we're making it possible to spend less time getting there and more time being there. If you want to be part of shaping the future of mobility, then read on! We are looking for a highly experienced and technically profound Principal Software Development Engineer to join our team. This pivotal role requires deep expertise in safety critical and modern software architectures and development methods, along with rigorous development processes, and mandatory compliance with leading industry safety standards (e.g., DO-178C, ISO 26262). Candidates must have a broad background with extensive experience in developing high-integrity systems across industries such as aerospace, automotive, rail, and defense. As the Principal Software Engineer, you will serve as a key technical leader, mentor, and architect, ensuring the highest levels of quality, reliability, and overall safety are met for the entire autonomous vehicle service, encompassing both the aircraft and essential ground systems. You will be supporting the Digital Products department within the Software and Simulation Systems (S3) organization. The Digital Products team is responsible for the complete life cycle for software and firmware used across our aircraft and ground systems. This is a hybrid work opportunity. At Wisk, this means working onsite at our Mountain View HQ office 3 days per week with flexibility for the remainder of the days. In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a lasting impact on aviation by establishing efficient, effective, modular, scalable, and safe architectural practices and operationalizing them through implementation in software products as part of Wisk's software team for an autonomous passenger-carrying eVTOL service. What you will do: In this role, situated within the S3 Organization, you will serve as a Principal Software Development Engineer dedicated to the development of a novel aircraft designed for the Advanced Air Mobility (AAM) market. You will define, architect, and champion the software framework for complex safety-critical embedded systems, ensuring scalability, performance, and adherence to design constraints. You will participate in, lead, and execute technical trade studies to evaluate design alternatives, technologies, and methodologies, ensuring the selection of optimal solutions with respect to performance, safety, and cost. You will be involved in technology maturation activities. You will ensure adherence to recognized safety standards, specifically DO-178C, DO-331, and DO-332, across the entire software lifecycle, encompassing planning, requirements specification, design, coding, and integration. You will participate and provide guidance during the requirements specification and design phases - decomposition of system requirements into traceable and verifiable high-level software requirements, and subsequently into low-level requirements or design models. You will collaborate with various software product teams and ensure software architecture and design principles laid out are followed through design and implementation of robust, high-performance embedded code primarily in C/C++ for RTOS-based environments. You will be part of software development and support activities during the software development life cycle. You will establish and continuously improve engineering best practices, coding standards, and requisite tools to maintain a highly reliable software development environment. You will provide technical mentorship and guidance to senior and staff engineers, serving as a software engineering domain expert within the organization. You will serve as a principal software liaison with the Systems Engineering, Hardware Engineering, and Safety teams to ensure the seamless integration and validation of the entire system. You will support internal and SOI audits. Requirements: BS in Computer Science, Electronics, or Electrical engineering, with a minimum of 15 years of substantial experience in the design and development of safety critical-software in the aerospace, automotive, rail or defence industries. Alternatively, 10 years of experience with a Master's degree, or with a PhD’s degree. Preferably, experience on multiple aerospace projects governed by DO-178C and its supplements. Demonstrated expertise in designing and developing software architecture for high-integrity, safety-critical embedded systems. Strong, practical proficiency in programming languages such as C, C++, and Assembly, along with embedded software toolchains. Experience with model-based software design and development techniques is a plus. Experience working with various Real-Time Operating Systems (RTOS). Solid understanding of ARINC 653, Integrated Modular Avionics (IMA), and various communication protocols relevant to avionics, including I2C, SPI, CAN, RS485, Arinc429, and Ethernet, as well as bootloaders and interrupt handling. Strong foundation of software and hardware, with a deep understanding of computer architecture, including microcontrollers, FPGA, memory management and peripherals. Ability to think from a system level perspective, and understand the relationship between software and hardware. Highly motivated, self-starting individual with strong communication skills and a positive team player. Strong theoretical and practical knowledge of design patterns, architectural patterns, and software modeling languages. Expertise in performing rigorous trade studies and making data-driven architectural decisions. Excellent communication skills with the ability to articulate complex design concepts to technical and non-technical stakeholders. title: Chef de service - Gestion et support des données PLM / Manager, PLM Data Management & Support
description_text: Chez Wisk, nous transformons l'avenir de la mobilité urbaine en proposant des vols sécuritaires, entièrement électriques et autonomes. Nous sommes une équipe passionnée qui travaille ensemble pour un avenir durable, en résolvant des problèmes à fort impact qui n'ont jamais été résolus auparavant. En proposant des vols quotidiens à portée de tous, nous faisons en sorte qu'il soit possible de passer moins de temps dans les transports et plus de temps à profiter. Si vous voulez faire partie du futur de la mobilité, poursuivez la lecture! Nous recherchons un(e) superviseur(e) stratégique et opérationnel(le) en gestion et soutien des données PLM pour piloter l'équipe responsable de l'intégrité, de la disponibilité et de l'optimisation de nos environnements de gestion du cycle de vie des produits (PLM) et de systèmes d'exécution de la production (MES). Vous assurerez la liaison entre les équipes de programme, d'ingénierie, de production et de TI, garantissant ainsi que nos données sur les produits constituent une référence unique fiable et optimisent l'efficacité de l'ensemble de la chaîne de valeur. Votre rôle ne se limite pas à la gestion d'une base de données ; vous êtes garant(e) de l’intégrité du référentiel numérique de nos produits. De la supervision de processus d'affaires complexes à la gestion d'une équipe de soutien technique réactive, votre objectif est de faire en sorte que nos systèmes Siemens PLM (Teamcenter), MES (OpCenter) et nos intégrations ERP (IFS) soient un véritable levier d'innovation, et non un frein. Il s’agit d’un poste hybride. Chez Wisk, cela signifie travailler sur place à notre bureau à Laval 3 jours par semaine avec une flexibilité pour le reste des jours. Responsabilités Leadership d'équipe Constituer et gérer une équipe internationale d'analystes de données PLM et de spécialistes du soutien, en favorisant une culture d'amélioration continue. Assurer la gestion du rendement individuel (définition des objectifs, élaboration des plans de développement, entrevues d'évaluation). Gouvernance stratégique des données et opérations de soutien Collaborer avec les bureaux de gestion de programmes et les autres intervenants clés pour aligner et gérer les priorités métiers. Établir et appliquer des normes de données afin d’assurer une qualité élevée des données. Valider la qualité de l'évolution et des mises à niveau du système. Élaborer une documentation complète destinée aux utilisateurs finaux afin d'optimiser l'adoption du système. Assurer le soutien de niveau 1 et veiller à ce que les problèmes techniques soient résolus dans les délais impartis (SLA). Collaborer avec les principaux intervenants du PLM pour maintenir et superviser une base de connaissances. Optimisation et conformité du système Assurer la performance de l’équipe de soutien PLM et du support de niveau 1, notamment le respect des SLA de réponse et de résolution, la gestion des priorités, des délais de traitement et l’exécution des opérations quotidiennes. Diriger et superviser le soutien opérationnel de la gestion de la configuration et des données, y compris les CN, MCN, ACN, CR et les revues de documents, en garantissant l'exactitude, la conformité et le respect des SLA de réponse et de traitement. Mettre en œuvre des mesures correctives, telles que la formation, l'amélioration des outils et une communication optimisée, afin de traiter et de résoudre les problèmes de qualité récurrents. Assurer la validation des exigences et des tests. Agirent en tant que contacts principaux pour les problèmes liés au support. Gérer les escalades, analyser les causes profondes et mettre en place des plans d'action correctifs. Gérer les flux de travail et les normes de prise en charge du service d'assistance. Vos tâches Prise en charge et triage PLM (niveau 1) : point de contact principal pour le soutien Siemens PLM. Trie et résout les problèmes de niveau 1 (accès, flux de travail, création de jeux de données, problèmes de mise en production, besoins de formation). Escalade les problèmes de niveau 2 et plus aux développeurs PLM ou à l'équipe informatique. Gère les billets de soutien détaillés et met à jour la base de connaissances. Gère la création de nouveaux utilisateurs et la résolution des problèmes d'autorisation. Aide les utilisateurs dans les flux de travail Teamcenter, les opérations de nomenclature et les exigences en matière de données. Valide et corrige les erreurs de données (par exemple: les révisions et la propriété). Déploiement et validation : Participe aux déploiements de PLM (Teamcenter et applications intégrées). Effectuer des tests de validation (« smoke tests ») et une validation initiale après les déploiements ou les mises à niveau. Coordonner la préparation du système avec les architectes, les développeurs et l’équipe TI. Tests et assurance qualité : Participer aux tests d’acceptation des utilisateurs (UAT) des nouvelles fonctionnalités et configurations. Collaborer avec les utilisateurs finaux pour valider le comportement du système par rapport aux besoins d'affaires, identifier les problèmes rencontrés et consigner les anomalies. Développer et maintenir les scripts de tests de régression pour Siemens PLM, en exécutant les cycles de tests avant les déploiements et les mises à niveau. Documenter les résultats et recommander des améliorations en fonction des anomalies récurrentes. Connaissances et formation : Refondre la base de connaissances PLM (articles, FAQ, guides) en fonction des problèmes courants et des commentaires des utilisateurs. Développer des modules d'apprentissage en ligne interactifs pour l'intégration, les meilleures pratiques et le dépannage. Animer des formations utilisateur en personne pour les mises à jour majeures ou sur demande, en utilisant des données réelles ou des simulations. Générer des rapports de conformité de formation (achèvement des formations en ligne). Indicateurs et amélioration : Suivre les indicateurs clés (volume de billets, délais de résolution) et les corrélations avec les efforts déployés dans la base de connaissances et les formations en ligne. Identifier les points de blocage récurrents des utilisateurs afin d’orienter les améliorations des processus PLM et la mise à jour du contenu de formation et de soutien. Exigences Qualifications et expérience Baccalauréat en génie et au minimum 8 ans d'expérience dans les processus PLM et la gestion des données. Expérience avérée des processus et des outils de configuration et de gestion des données PLM, notamment dans le secteur aérospatial. Expérience pratique des systèmes PLM dans le contexte de la conception et de la fabrication aérospatiales. Connaissance approfondie et appliquée des méthodologies CAO et PLM complexes. Maîtrise des systèmes CAO et PLM, comme NX et Teamcenter. Leadership et gestion d'équipe Capacité démontrée à définir et à gérer l'exécution des projets d'équipe, conformément aux priorités du programme. Expérience de la direction d'équipes ayant mis en œuvre avec succès les meilleures pratiques d'ingénierie pour assurer la réussite du programme. Compétences et qualités Excellentes compétences en gestion des intervenants, avec la capacité d'influencer sans autorité hiérarchique. Excellentes aptitudes en communication et une forte capacité à collaborer efficacement au sein d’équipes multidisciplinaires. Nous offrons un ensemble complet d'avantages incluant une assurance médicale, dentaire, visuelle et une assurance-vie (prise en charge à 100 % de la prime pour les employés par l’entreprise), 9 vendredis libres pour les employés à temps plein tout au long de l'année, 20 jours de congés payés, 10 jours fériés, un plan d’épargne-retraite avec une contribution de l’entreprise à 100 % jusqu'à 8 % de vos revenus admissibles et une aide à la formation. En plus, nous proposons divers avantages, tels que des leçons de pilotage, des allocations bien-être, des collations, des boissons, un bonus pour recommandation d’employés, une contribution aux dons de charité, un programme de bonus pour les brevets, et bien plus encore. La maîtrise de l'anglais est requise pour ce poste afin de développer notre produit et de servir nos clients, dont la majorité ou la totalité se trouvent à l'extérieur du Québec, ainsi que pour communiquer avec des partenaires et des collègues situés aux États-Unis, en Pologne, en Australie, en Nouvelle-Zélande et au Canada. Les informations techniques provenant de ces pays sont disponibles en anglais seulement. Une formation en français est offerte à tout titulaire d'un poste permanent au Québec qui ne possède pas une bonne connaissance du français. We are a passionate team working together toward a sustainable future, solving high-impact problems that have never been solved before. By delivering everyday flight for everyone, we're making it possible to spend less time getting there and more time being there. If you want to be part of shaping the future of mobility, then read on! We are looking for a strategic and hands-on PLM Data Management & Support Supervisor to lead the team responsible for the integrity, availability, and optimization of our Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) and Manufacturing Execution System (MES) environments. You will bridge the gap between program, engineering, manufacturing, and IT, ensuring that our product data is a "single source of truth" that drives efficiency across the entire value chain. You aren’t just managing a database; you are managing the quality of the digital thread of our products. From overseeing complex business processes to leading a high-response technical support team, your goal is to ensure our Siemens PLM (Teamcenter), MES (OpCenter) systems and ERP (IFS) integrations are a seamless enabler of innovation rather than a bottleneck. This is a hybrid work opportunity. At Wisk, this means working onsite at our Laval office 3 days per week with flexibility for the remainder of the days. Responsibilities Team Leadership Build and manage a global team of PLM data analysts and support specialists, fostering a culture of continuous improvement. Owns individual performance management (goal setting, development plans, performance reviews). Strategic Data Governance & Support Operations Collaborate with Program Management offices and other key stakeholders to align and manage business priorities. Establish and enforce data standards to ensure high data quality. Validating the quality of the system evolution and upgrades. Develop comprehensive documentation for end-users to maximize system adoption. Own the level 1 support desk, ensuring technical issues are resolved within established SLAs. Collaborate with key PLM stakeholders to maintain and oversee a knowledge base database. System Optimization & Compliance Accountable for PLM Support and Level 1 Helpdesk team performance, including response and resolution SLAs, review turnaround times, workload prioritization, and day-to-day execution. Leads and oversees Configuration and data Management operational support, including CN, MCN, ACN, CR, and document reviews, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and adherence to response and turnaround SLAs. Implement corrective actions, such as training, tool enhancements, and improved communication, to address and resolve recurring quality issues. Leads requirements validation and testing activities. Acts as the primary POC for support-related issues. Owns escalations, root cause analysis, and corrective action plans. Owns helpdesk workflows and intake standards. What your team will do: PLM Support & Triage (L1): Serve as the primary contact for all Siemens PLM support. Triage and resolve Level 1 issues (access, workflow, dataset creation, release problems, training needs). Escalate Level 2+ issues to PLM developers or IT. Maintain detailed support tickets and update the knowledge base. Handle new user creation and permission troubleshooting. Assist users with Teamcenter workflows, BOM operations, and data requirements. Validate and correct data errors (e.g., revisions, ownership). Deployment & Validation: Assist with PLM deployments (Teamcenter and integrated apps). Perform smoke testing and initial validation post-deployment/upgrade. Coordinate system readiness with architects, developers, and IT. Testing & Quality Assurance: Participate in UAT for new features and configurations. Work with end users to validate system behavior against business needs, capture issues, and log defects. Develop and maintain regression test scripts for Siemens PLM, executing cycles before deployments and upgrades. Document results and recommend improvements based on recurring defects. Knowledge & Training: Revamp the PLM knowledge base (articles, FAQs, guides) based on common issues and user feedback. Develop interactive e-learning modules for onboarding, best practices, and troubleshooting. Facilitate in-person user training for major releases or upon request, using real-world data/simulations. Generate training compliance reports (e-learning completion). Metrics & Improvement: Monitor key metrics (ticket volume, resolution time) and correlate with knowledge base/e-learning efforts. Identify recurring user pain points to inform PLM Process Team improvements and content updates. Requirements Qualifications & Experience Bachelor's degree in Engineering and a minimum of 8 years of experience in PLM Processes and Data Management. Demonstrated experience in PLM Configuration and Data Management processes and tools, specifically within the aerospace sector. Hands-on expertise with PLM systems in the context of aerospace design and manufacturing. Deep, applied knowledge of complex CAD and PLM methodologies. Familiarity with CAD and PLM systems, such as NX and Teamcenter. Leadership & Team Management Proven ability to define and manage team execution, aligning with program priorities. Experience leading teams that successfully implemented engineering best practices to achieve program success. Skills & Attributes Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence others without formal authority. Effective communication skills and a collaborative team player mindset. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, and life insurance (company pays 100% of employee premium), 9 off Fridays for full time employees throughout the year, 20 days of PTO, 10 company holidays, a retirement savings plan with 100% company matching up to 8% of your eligible earnings and education assistance. And on top of all that, we also offer a variety of perks such as flight lessons, wellness allowances, snacks, drinks, employee referral bonus, charitable giving match, patent bonus program, and more. English is required for this position in order to develop our product and eventually serve our customers, most or all of whom are located outside Quebec, as well as to communicate with partners and colleagues located in the United States, Poland, Australia, New Zealand, and Canada. The role is critical to ensuring the integrity, traceability, and compliance of all software and firmware configuration items throughout the product lifecycle, adhering to strict industry standards (e.g., DO-178C, DO-254). You will be responsible for defining and enforcing CM policies and managing the change control process. You will be part of the Software Foundation team within the Software, Simulation and Systems (S3) organization. The Software Foundation team is responsible for developing common software plans, standards, processes, tools and software certification for all aircraft and ground systems software. This is a hybrid work opportunity. At Wisk, this means working onsite at our Mountain View HQ office 3 days per week with flexibility for the remainder of the days. In this role you will have the opportunity to leave a lasting impact on aviation by setting up operational, efficient, and effective CM processes and policies being a part of the Software Foundation team for an autonomous passenger carrying eVTOL service. What you will do: As part of the S3 Organization, you will act as a CM Specialist for a novel type of aircraft development targeting the autonomous UAM market. You will define and own CM plan and processes (configuration identification, baseline management, change control). You will define, create, and control baselines (development, integration, release) for all software configuration items. You will standardize Git/GitLab workflows (branching, MR approvals, tagging, releases). You will ensure reproducible builds and controlled toolchains. You will govern release configuration items across teams in the S3 organization. You will establish and support change control (PR/CR/CCB), including logging and reviewing all proposed changes to baselined configuration items, as well as ensure proper impact assessment and authorization of implementation. You will ensure traceability across requirements, code, and tests. You will ensure the accurate recording and reporting of the status of all CIs, including their approval status, version history, and change implementation progress. Solid knowledge of DO-178C (or equivalent). Strong Git-based workflow experience (e.g., GitLab). Experience defining and governing processes across multiple teams. Understanding of traceability, baseline management, and change control objectives. Excellent communication, interpersonal, presentation skills and able to work with a cross functional team. Desired: Experience with CI/CD and build reproducibility. Experience in setting up and managing a continuous integration/continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipeline for safety-critical systems. Familiarity with requirements and life cycle data management tools. Experience in working across multiple systems on a single product. title: Senior Manager, Flight Dynamics
description_text: At Wisk, we're transforming the future of urban mobility through safe, all-electric, autonomous flight. We are a passionate team working together toward a sustainable future, solving high-impact problems that have never been solved before. By delivering everyday flight for everyone, we're making it possible to spend less time getting there and more time being there. If you want to be part of shaping the future of mobility, then read on! We are looking for a Senior Manager of Flight Dynamics to join our team. The goal of this role at Wisk is to bring experience, skill and capability to the Flight Dynamics team working on the Wisk Gen 6 aircraft team that is bringing a commercial autonomous air taxi to the market. This is a hybrid work opportunity. At Wisk, this means working onsite at our Mountain View HQ 3 days per week with flexibility for the remainder of the days. As part of the Gen 6 aircraft team organization you lead the Flight Dynamics team, responsible for Aircraft modelling, simulation and analysis, covering aircraft performance, stability and control and 6DOF real-time simulation. A successful candidate will have direct hands-on experience developing and building type certified aircraft, from initial concept through flight testing and certification, with the technical breadth necessary to interface with all engineering functions such as Systems & Safety Engineering, Hardware, Software, Manufacturing, Test, and Certification. In this role, the candidate must be a highly motivated, self-starting individual with a broad set of skills and never afraid of facing complex technical challenges. Role Responsibilities: Lead a highly skilled engineering team consisting of engineers covering Stability and Control, Vehicle Performance, Vehicle 6DOF and Systems modelling. Define the end-to-end modeling and simulation strategy for Flight Dynamics, not limited to 6DOF, Vehicle stability & trim analysis, Aircraft Performance, and Systems Integration. Develop and maintain Wisk’s in-house tools to perform linear and non-linear stability and control analysis, estimate critical performance parameters, such as range, stall speeds, optimized climb profiles, aerodynamics characteristics, and validate our tools with test data from wind tunnels, subscale aircraft and flight test. Capture the Flight Physics Modelling and Simulation Methodology for Flight Dynamics Define the Flight Dynamics Model Validation Strategy including Closed Loop System Validation, incorporation of Flight test and wind tunnel corrections. Set specific goals and objectives for each model to build up to a fully certifiable model set (where appropriate). This role requires driving alignment across Flight Physics, GNC, System Integration, and Flight test teams. Establish KPP/Metrics to show Models validation progress and quantify model maturity. Drive technical consensus among other Aircraft SMEs to align on Modelling requirements and expectations across all product teams. Set standards for documentation of Aircraft level modelling, documentation, and configuration control of the models vs the overall aircraft design baseline and vehicle and systems description documents. Produce and Review Certification Detailed Design Standards, White Papers and Certification Plans to support certification compliance showing against with FAR Part 21/23/27/91/135 requirements Define MDO opportunities, and in-line with SMEs and Chief Engineer office, roll out MDO for Flight Physics and Aircraft Configuration Design as a way to conduct System-Level Trade Studies. Spearhead the adoption of AI-driven surrogate modeling and GPU accelerated solvers (CUDA) to achieve real-time aero-performance analysis. Integrate high-priority, cross functional roadblocks identified during Gen 6 flight testing, particularly those occurring at the intersection of non-linear aerodynamics and autonomous control laws. Requirements: 15 years of industry experience in aircraft and/or rotorcraft development, with a Bachelor’s degree; or 12 years and a Master’s degree; or a PhD with 8 years experience; or equivalent experience. Understanding of rotorcraft and multirotor aircraft aerodynamics and vehicle stability in all modes of flight. Experience with commercial aviation development and Certification as well as rapid development of novel, complex systems. Familiarity with aerodynamic data base creation and tools such as CFD, wind tunnel testing, data processing, and flight matching. Experience with aerodynamic design and analysis of aircraft to meet performance goals Experience creating vehicle 6DOF simulation models that support certification compliance showing. Experience with complex modelling frameworks Able to run multiple objective optimizations and trade-off studies for complex aircraft Excellent written and verbal communication skills. You are excited to learn about the team, and leverage the hard learned lessons of the past 14+ years of eVTOL development here at Wisk. Desired: Aeromechanics and/or rotor dynamics experience Familiarity with Matlab/Simulink, Python and C/C++ Familiarity with Jira, Git, or other software product lifecycle management tools Been part of a program, or programs, working on a clean sheet novel aircraft from concept design to certification. Electric engine performance analysis and compliance showing Previous FAA Part 23/25 and/or Part 27 certification program experience preferred FAA Flight Analyst Designated Engineering Representative (DER) for Part 23/25/27 Knowledge of rotorcraft performance and the theories associated with propeller and rotor-driven propulsion Job Type: Full-time Pay range the company expects to pay: $218,000 - $272,000 per year - The starting base salary within this range for this role varies based on factors such as your geographical location, and your relevant job-related experience that is consistent with a business necessity. title: Vice President of People
description_text: At Wisk, we're transforming the future of urban mobility through safe, all-electric, autonomous flight. We are a passionate team working together toward a sustainable future, solving high-impact problems that have never been solved before. By delivering everyday flight for everyone, we're making it possible to spend less time getting there and more time being there. If you want to be part of shaping the future of mobility, then read on! At Wisk, we’re transforming the future of urban mobility through safe, all-electric, autonomous flight. We are a passionate team working together toward a sustainable future, solving high-impact problems that have never been solved before. By delivering everyday flight for everyone, we’re making it possible to spend less time getting there and more time being there. If you want to be part of shaping the future of mobility, then read on! We are looking for a visionary, empathetic and operationally focused VP of People to lead our People department and champion our most important asset: our talent. As a member of the leadership team, you will be a champion and driver of company culture, ensure that our organizational structure and talent strategy best support our business goals, and drive HR operational excellence to help our company operate efficiently and effectively. The ideal candidate isn’t just an HR expert – they are a strategic partner who understands how a high-performing culture drives results. You will oversee everything from talent acquisition and total rewards to HR operations and organizational development. This is an incredible opportunity to come work for a truly innovative global aviation company in the heart of the Bay Area! This is a hybrid work opportunity, which means working onsite at our headquarters at least 3 days per week, with flexibility for the remainder of the days. This position reports directly to the CEO. Core responsibilities include: Strategic Leadership & Partnership: Partner with the leadership team to align people strategies with business objectives. Lead organizational design and workforce planning to support our company on our path to commercialization. You are comfortable being a voice at the table, navigating and communicating complex situations directly and with empathy. You can support and coach at all levels within the organization. Culture & Engagement: With the CEO, be one of the biggest champions of our culture. Ensure our culture is aligned to our vision, mission and values. Weave our values and leadership competencies into the daily fabric of our company and find ways to strengthen our culture. The goal of this team at Wisk is to design, implement and analyze controls algorithms, state estimation methods, and self-flying autonomous software. You will model and create software that runs on prototype and full scale airplanes, as well as ultimately supporting the flight tests of those new algorithms. Additionally, you will be part of the Aircraft IPT organization, which is responsible for developing and delivering a safe autonomous flight vehicle by following standard aviation certification processes, performing rigorous testing and utilizing formal controls analysis techniques. You will support problem solving activities following flight tests on our Subscale prototype vehicle, in which you will improve control law algorithms after reviewing the flight test data. You will help coordinate analysis activities to support verification of design requirements for satisfying software certification objectives. You will take part in developing new GNC tools using software best practices in MATLAB/Simulink In some areas, you will be working closely with the Model-Based Design Team, contributing to software development and verification activities for developing high-integrity software. You will support SIL/HIL and flight test operations activities in debugging anomalies and issues. You have applied knowledge of fixed wing and/or rotorcraft aerodynamics and aircraft flight dynamics. You have a strong simulation background for testing your control laws in a 6DOF environment. title: Staff Electrical Engineer, BMS
description_text: At Wisk, we're transforming the future of urban mobility through safe, all-electric, autonomous flight. We are a passionate team working together toward a sustainable future, solving high-impact problems that have never been solved before. By delivering everyday flight for everyone, we're making it possible to spend less time getting there and more time being there. If you want to be part of shaping the future of mobility, then read on! Join the Future of Autonomous Flight at Wisk Aero! Are you ready to design the core power systems for the next generation of autonomous aviation? Wisk Aero is pioneering Urban Air Mobility (UAM), and we're seeking a visionary Staff Hardware BMS Electrical Engineer to join our Energy Storage and Power Distribution System (ESPS) Integrated Product Team (IPT). This is more than a job—it's a chance to architect the electronic heart of our Gen 6 eVTOL aircraft, ensuring the highest standards of safety and performance. What You'll Build As a key member of the Battery Management System (BMS) team, you will be directly responsible for the design, development, and certification of the cutting-edge electronic assemblies that control and monitor our high and low-voltage battery systems, power distribution, and circuit protections. Your work is fundamental to the safety and success of our autonomous aircraft. Key Responsibilities & Impact Lead Design & Architecture: Drive the design and development of critical Battery Management System hardware components for our Gen 6 aircraft. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with Systems, Mechanical, Test, and Safety engineering teams to ensure a robust, integrated, and safe design from concept through implementation. High-Stakes Problem Solving: Leverage your hands-on expertise to solve complex, safety-critical system challenges in design, integration, and rigorous testing. Lifecycle Management: Plan and execute the design lifecycle, coordinating design, verification, and procurement to ensure on-schedule delivery for our groundbreaking aircraft. Strategic & Mentorship Role: Contribute to architectural decisions, lead design reviews, define technical requirements, assist in strategic outsourcing, and participate in shaping the future of the team through new-hire interviews. Certification Excellence: Support essential documentation and verification efforts crucial for the successful certification of the Gen 6 aircraft. What You'll Bring B.S. in Electrical Engineering with at least 8 years of relevant experience, or an M.S. with 6 years of experience. Proven background in the aerospace or automotive industries, specializing in high-reliability, safety-critical systems. Deep BMS Mastery: Applied expertise in Battery Management Systems, High Voltage (HV) systems, and both analog and digital electronics. PCB/PCBA Design Proficiency: Extensive experience with PCBA design tools for schematic capture and layout, preferably with the Altium design suite. Embedded Systems Expertise: Hands-on experience implementing microprocessor, microcontrollers, and FPGAs at the hardware, software, and firmware levels. Solid understanding of embedded systems concepts, including interconnect, sensor, and power conversion board design. Bonus Experience: Familiarity with rigorous aerospace development, verification, and documentation standards such as DO-254 and DO-160G. Additional Details A Unique Challenge: Work on a novel type of aircraft development targeting the autonomous Urban Air Mobility (UAM) market. Onsite Work: This role will be an onsite (5 days a week) position in our Mountain View Headquarter Travel: This role involves up to 25% travel, supporting integration and testing efforts. Compensation and Benefits: In addition to offering a great work environment and the opportunity to be part of the team making electric autonomous flight a reality, at Wisk, we offer excellent total rewards which include a competitive base salary, annual bonus, long term incentive, 401K, health benefits and much more. Job Type: Full-time Pay Range the Company expects to pay: $173,069 - $203,610 per year - The starting base salary within this range for this role varies based on factors such as your geographical location, and your relevant job-related experience that is consistent with a business necessity. Wisk HQ is located in Mountain View just a couple of hundred feet from Shoreline Lake and its surrounding walking/jogging trails. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, and life insurance, 9 off Fridays for full-time employees throughout the year, flexible vacation time off, 11 company holidays, a 401(k) plan with 50% company matching vested immediately, commuter assistance, and education assistance. And on top of all that, we also offer a variety of perks such as free catered lunches, flight lessons, wellness allowances, snacks, drinks, employee referral bonus, charitable giving match, patent bonus program, and more.
